title: hotato assertion run, schema assert.v1
description: The output of `hotato assert`: every assertion in an assertions.yaml, evaluated against a call's transcript/spans/timing. HONESTY INVARIANT, structural: every result carries `deterministic: true` -- all five assertion kinds (phrase, pii, policy, tool_call, outcome) are pure regex/checksum/span-lookup, never a model call. `summary` SPLITS deterministic from judge counts and NEVER emits a merged percentage or an overall_score field; a judge (model-scored) kind is a separate, quarantined capability not built here, so summary.judge is always {pass:0, fail:0}. Additive-only: new result fields and new kinds may appear without a version bump; a consumer must ignore unknown fields.

exit_code.description: Under the default inconclusive_policy 'report': 1 if any result's status is FAIL, 2 for a malformed assertions file / bad input (raised before any result exists), 0 otherwise -- an INCONCLUSIVE result never forces exit 1 by itself. Two opt-in policies change how INCONCLUSIVE gates (see inconclusive_policy): 'fail' makes an INCONCLUSIVE result exit 1 like a FAIL; 'refuse' makes an INCONCLUSIVE result exit 2 (a refusal to return a verdict when required input is missing), taking precedence over a FAIL.

inconclusive_policy.description: OPTIONAL, additive. The gating policy actually applied to this run (how an INCONCLUSIVE result -- absent required input, never a failed judgment -- affects exit_code). 'report' (default): INCONCLUSIVE never forces a non-zero exit; the historical, backward-compatible behavior. 'fail': INCONCLUSIVE gates like a FAIL (exit 1). 'refuse': INCONCLUSIVE refuses a verdict (exit 2), taking precedence over a FAIL. Absent on envelopes built before this field existed; a consumer must not require it.

judge.description: Always {pass: 0, fail: 0} in this build: a judge (model-scored) assertion kind is a separate, quarantined capability, not built here.

note.description: Always states how many judge-scored assertions ran (0, in this build).

kind.description: The original five: phrase (regex over the transcript), pii (regex/checksum detectors, must_not_leak), policy (a named offline rule pack), tool_call (ingested voice_trace.v1 spans only, never transcript text), outcome (all_of/any_of deterministic sub-predicates as a met/of fraction). The Phase-1 expanded deterministic kinds -- additive, all deterministic:true, each reading the AUTHENTICATED trace spans / a post-call state adapter / numeric timing, never the agent's spoken claim: tool_result (Authority 1: a tool span's result matches expected fields), tool_error (Authority 1: a tool span errored), state (Authority 2: a post-call state-adapter record matches), state_change (Authority 2: a before/after state delta), handoff (a transfer span occurred), dtmf (expected DTMF digits in the trace), termination (the call ended in the expected way), latency (a span latency_ms or a timing field is <= a threshold), timing_contract (an embedded .hotato timing contract re-verifies via `contract verify`), entity_accuracy (critical entities present/correct vs a supplied reference by deterministic string match, NOT WER), sequence (matchers hold in trace order), count (a span/phrase occurs N times). human_rubric/judge_rubric are the model-judged lane -- NAMED but quarantined until Phase 3; in this build their evaluator deterministically returns INCONCLUSIVE ('rubric engine not built (Phase 3)') and no LLM path is built. INVARIANT: the Authority 1 & 2 kinds (tool_result, tool_error, state, state_change) have no transcript read and no model code path, so an agent's spoken claim can never satisfy one.

deterministic.description: HONESTY INVARIANT: always true for these five kinds -- no model call ever backs a result here, including an INCONCLUSIVE one (which reflects absent required input, not a non-deterministic judgment).

of.description: outcome kind only: total sub-predicates evaluated.

span_ids.description: tool_call kind: the exact voice_trace.v1 span(s) (synthetic stable ids, s_<index>) the result is grounded in.

hits.description: pii kind: which detector fired on which transcript turn. NEVER carries the matched raw text -- see redacted_transcript for the redacted artifact.

redacted_transcript.description: pii kind: the full transcript with every detector hit replaced by [REDACTED]. The raw PII text is never present anywhere in an assert.v1 result.

matched_rules.description: policy kind: which named rules matched (banned language present, or a required disclosure missing).

pack.description: policy kind: the {name, version} of the rule pack evaluated, for byte-replayability.

measured_ms.description: latency kind (trace source): the slowest matching span's latency_ms the threshold was checked against.

measured.description: latency kind (timing source): the numeric timing-field value the threshold was checked against.

observed.description: count kind: how many matching spans/phrase-turns were observed.

delta.description: state_change kind: the {field, before, after} values read from the before/after state-adapter snapshots.

contracts.description: timing_contract kind: the {total, passed} count from re-verifying the referenced .hotato bundle(s).
